structured.description: A class called "Calibration" that performs calibration for a robotic arm using a camera attached to it. The code first imports necessary packages, including numpy, scipy, and pybullet. It then defines functions for detecting corners in an image, calculating the error between real and virtual corners, and minimizing the error function using the Nelder-Mead method to find the optimal parameters for the camera intrinsic matrix. The code also includes a summary of the calibration process and writes the results to a file.

items.description: Performs calibration of a kinova cube robot using pybullet library and OpenCV for image processing. It detects green corners, compares them with real ones, calculates errors and minimizes them through a non-linear optimization algorithm.

attributes.name: width
attributes.description: 1280 pixels, which represents the width of the camera image used for calibration.

attributes.name: error
attributes.description: 0 at first, then it gets updated with the error calculation during the calibration process. It represents the total error between the estimated and real positions of the corners.

attributes.name: real_corners
attributes.description: 4D, representing the real corners of the object in the image. It contains the coordinates of the real corners detected using the green rectangle detection algorithm.

attributes.name: robot_id
attributes.description: Used to identify the specific robot being calibrated during the process.

attributes.name: observation_angles
attributes.description: 7-dimensional, containing the angles for each joint in a robot in radians.

items.name: Calibrator

items.description: Sets the parameters for a calibration process, such as camera intrinsic and extrinsic parameters, and the angles of observation.
items.name: __init__

items.description: Determines whether a set of 4 points can be formed into a rectangle based on the cosine of the angles between adjacent pairs of points. If all cosine values are below a threshold (default 0.1), it returns True, otherwise it returns False.

params.name: approx
params.description: Used to represent an approximate rectangle.

returns.description: True if the given approximate rectangle is a rectangle and False otherwise.

items.name: is_rectangle

items.description: Calculates the angle between two points in a 2D space based on their distances and angles relative to a reference point.

params.name: pt1
params.description: Representing a point on a two-dimensional plane.

params.name: pt2
params.description: Represented by a tuple containing the x and y coordinates, respectively.

params.name: pt0
params.description: Represented as a point (x,y) value.

returns.description: The angle between two points based on their distances and directions from a reference point.

items.name: angle

items.description: Detects green regions in an image using HSV color space, then finds contours within those regions and extracts corner points.

params.name: image
params.description: Used to represent the input image for corner detection.

returns.description: A collection of corner points detected from an image using Hough transform.

items.name: detect_green_corners

items.description: Computes the distance between real and virtual corners, calculates the error, and returns the sum of errors.

params.name: params
params.description: 7-dimensional, containing the 3 real corners and 4 virtual corners of an object.

returns.description: 1000 times the distance between the real and virtual corners of a rectangle.

items.name: error_function

items.description: In the Calibrator class calculates the distance between real and virtual corners, checks for errors, and updates the errors array.

params.name: params
params.description: 7-dimensional, containing 3 real values, followed by 4 integer indices.

returns.description: The sum of the norms of the differences between the detected virtual corners and the real corners.

items.name: error_function2

items.description: Performs corner detection and matching between a set of real corners detected by a previous step, and a set of virtual corners obtained from a green-texture image processed by the `cvPose2BulletView` method. The function calculates the errors between the real and virtual corners, and returns the sum of these errors as an integer value.

params.name: params
params.description: Used to store the camera translation value.

returns.description: Not a valid error value, indicating an error in the code.

items.name: error_function3

items.description: Performs camera calibration for both Kinova and Pybullet cameras. It detects rectangles in the images, computes offsets for the real corners, and updates the position of the camera parameters.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: Used to represent the left-hand side (LHS) of the calibration equation, which is the image of the object in the robot's camera.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot for which the calibration is performed.

items.name: calibrate

items.description:
Computes the optimal position and orientation of a robot based on image data.
It uses a minimization algorithm to find the solution that reduces an error function.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: 2D array that stores the raw image captured by the Kinova camera.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot being calibrated.

items.name: calibrate2

items.description: Within the Calibrator class takes robot ID, an image from Kinova, and initial camera parameters as inputs. It then computes the fixed projection matrix using the green corners detection method and minimizes the error function to find the optimal translation vector for the camera. Finally, it displays the detected corners in both Kinova and Pybullet images.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to retrieve link state data from the Kinova robot controller.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: A grayscale image representing the left side camera view.

items.name: calibrate3

items.description: Computes the view matrix and projection matrix for a camera using a FOV, aspect ratio, near plane, and far plane. It then rotates the image and converts it to BGR format before returning it.

returns.description: A RGB image represented as a numpy array.

items.name: read_camera

items.description: Computes and returns the fixed camera matrix and view matrix for a given camera, based on its intrinsic and extrinsic parameters.

returns.description: A grayscale image representing the scene captured by a camera.

items.name: read_camera_fixed

items.description: Within the `Calibrator` class detects and matches green corners between two images, calculating the distance between them using norms, and returns the matched corners and errors in a matrix format.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot to which the code is applied.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: An image captured by a camera linked to a robot.

items.name: cube_test

items.description: Within the Calibrator class performs rectangle detection on two images: one from a Pybullert camera and another from a Kinova camera. It matches virtual corners with real corners using a descriptor-based approach and calculates errors between the two sets of corners.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to specify the robot ID for which the rectangle detection should be performed.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: Passed as an argument to the function for testing purposes.

items.name: square_test

items.description: Within the Calibrator class retrieves images from a Kinova robot and a camera, moves the robot's joints to specific angles, and saves the obtained color images to a directory.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the specific robot being controlled.

params.name: kinova_proxy
params.description: Used to control the movement of a kinova arm.

params.name: camera_proxy
params.description: Used to get all images from the camera.

items.name: get_kinova_images

items.description: Calculates the error between real and virtual corners in a 2D image, given by a set of parameters. It returns the total error as a scalar value.

params.name: params
params.description: 3-dimensional, containing the following values: optical center x pixels, optical center y pixels, and focal length in pixels.

returns.description: The sum of the absolute differences between the virtual corners and real corners found using SIFT feature detection and matching.

items.name: error_function4_square

items.description: Calculates the distance between real and virtual corners and returns the error. It also detects green corners from an image and compares them with the expected ones.

params.name: params
params.description: Passed as a single argument to the function. It contains two elements: `optical_center_x_pixels` and `optical_center_y_pixels`, which are the coordinates of the optical center in pixels.

returns.description: 2000 if there are at least 1000 differences between the real and virtual corners, otherwise it returns the number of differences.

items.name: error_function4_cube

items.description: Within the `Calibrator` class minimizes an error function to find the optimal parameters for a calibration process using the Nelder-Mead method.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Assigned the value passed to the function as an argument, serving as an identifier for the robot being calibrated.

items.name: calibrate4

structured.description:
an object of the class `Robot` from the `pybullet` library, which is a Python interface to the Bullet physics engine. The code subscribes to data updates from a `JoystickAdapter` interface and uses the received data to update the position and orientation of the robot's end effector (gripper). The code also handles button presses and moves the robot's gripper based on the button presses.

The code defines several methods for handling the data updates, including `JoystickAdapter_sendData`, which is called whenever new data is received from the joystick adapter, and `move_mode`, `home`, `X_axis`, `Y_axis`, `Z_axis`, and `gripper` buttons, which are handled based on their respective names.

Overall, the code provides a simple example of how to use the `pybullet` library to control a robot's end effector using data from a joystick adapter.

items.description: Manages a robot's movements and actions based on a set of instructions received from an external device. It updates the robot's position, orientation, and gripper state according to the instructions and handles buttons presses and axis inputs.

attributes.name: Period
attributes.description: 3, indicating that the worker has a period of 3 seconds between movements.

attributes.name: rgb
attributes.description: 3-element tuple containing red, green, and blue color values for the worker's color representation.

attributes.name: startup_check
attributes.description: 4 by default, indicating that it checks for 4 specific cases when starting up the worker.

attributes.name: physicsClient
attributes.description: Used to interact with the PyRobot2 physics engine to control the robot's movement and orientation. It allows for reading and setting of joint angles, axis positions, and button inputs.

attributes.name: plane
attributes.description: Used to store the target position of the robot's end effector in 3D space, represented as a list of 3 elements (x, y, z) representing the position of the end effector in the world coordinate system.

attributes.name: table_id
attributes.description: 4 in this specific implementation, indicating that the worker is specifically designed for table-based manipulation tasks.

attributes.name: robot_urdf
attributes.description: 3D robot model represented as a UrDF (Universal Robot Description Format) file, which defines the geometry and kinematics of the robot.

attributes.name: robot_launch_pos
attributes.description: Used to store the position of the robot's end effector after a successful launch.

attributes.name: robot_launch_orien
attributes.description: Used to store the orientation of the robot's end effector relative to its base frame. It is updated based on user inputs from the joy controller and button presses.

attributes.name: end_effector_link_index
attributes.description: 0-based indexing for the end effector joints of the robot, indicating which joint the end effector is attached to.

attributes.name: home_angles
attributes.description: 4D, with shape `(7,)` where each row represents a joint angle for the robot's end effector, and each column represents the corresponding joint angle for the gripper. It stores the initial angles for the robot and gripper when `home` mode is selected.

attributes.name: observation_angles
attributes.description: Used to store the target angles for each joint of the robot, which are calculated based on the user input from the joy controller.

attributes.name: observation_angles_2
attributes.description: 4D, representing the angles of the robot's joints for observation 2. It stores the angles of the robot's joints based on the data from the IMU sensors.

attributes.name: observation_angles_3
attributes.description: 3-dimensional, representing the angles for joints 1 to 7 in the robot's end effector.

attributes.name: observation_angles_cube
attributes.description: Used to store the angles of the cube's joints based on the observation of the user's input.

attributes.name: robot_id
attributes.description: 4 by default, which corresponds to the ID of the robot being controlled by the worker.

attributes.name: pybullet_cup
attributes.description: A reference to the path of the cup file of the worker in PyBullet environment.

attributes.name: square
attributes.description: 1 by default, which represents the square of the joint angle movement range for each axis.

attributes.name: hilo_lectura
attributes.description: 126 characters long. It contains a Python code snippet that runs when the `SpecificWorker` is created, setting up the robot's joint angles and button states based on user input.

attributes.name: readDataFromProxy
attributes.description: Used to read data from a proxy server, which receives data from the real robot. The attribute reads the data from the proxy server and passes it to the worker's internal state for processing.

attributes.name: target_angles
attributes.description: Used to store the target angles for each joint of the robot in a specific mode.

attributes.name: target_position
attributes.description: Used to store the target position of the robot's end effector, which represents the desired position of the robot's arm to reach in the workspace.

attributes.name: target_orientation
attributes.description: Used to store the target orientation of the robot, which is updated based on the input from the joystick and other sensors.

attributes.name: target_velocities
attributes.description: 4D, with shape `(3, 3, 2, 2)`, representing the target velocities for each joint in the robot's end effector in the two orientations.

attributes.name: joy_selected_joint
attributes.description: 0-based indexing of a joint in the robot's arm, indicating which joint the user has selected using the joystick.

attributes.name: move_mode
attributes.description: 0-based indexing for different move modes, such as "home", "pick", or "delivery". It keeps track of the current move mode.

attributes.name: n_rotations
attributes.description: 4 in this example, indicating that the worker has 4 rotational degrees of freedom (i.e., 3 rotations around the x, y, and z axes).

attributes.name: ext_joints
attributes.description: Used to store the selected joint values for the external joystick. It contains the joint values selected by the user through the external joystick, which are used to control the robot's movement and orientation.

attributes.name: kinovaarm_proxy
attributes.description: Used to handle the robot's kinematic movements by translating the joint angles into real-world robot movements.

attributes.name: ext_gripper
attributes.description: 0 by default, which means that the gripper does not have any external force feedback.

attributes.name: posesTimes
attributes.description: 4D, representing the robot's position and orientation in a list format, where each element represents one dimension (position or orientation) over time.

attributes.name: poses
attributes.description: 3D position and orientation vectors for the robot's end effector, which are used to define the robot's position and orientation in space.

attributes.name: timestamp
attributes.description: Used to represent the timestamp (in seconds) when the worker was created.

attributes.name: timer
attributes.description: 0 by default, representing the time it takes for the worker to complete its task.

attributes.name: compute
attributes.description: 4-dimensional tensor that holds the result of the computation of the worker's actions based on the input data from the robot and the environment.

attributes.name: timer2
attributes.description: 4 seconds by default, indicating how long the worker will wait before polling for new data from the robot again.

attributes.name: movePybulletWithExternalVel
attributes.description: Defined as a function that moves the robot using PyBullet and external velocity inputs.

attributes.name: joint_speeds
attributes.description: Used to store the joint speeds for the robot's end effector in a specific mode.

attributes.name: gains
attributes.description: A set of gains for each axis, representing the amount of movement per step in each direction.

attributes.name: speeds
attributes.description: 4D, with shape (7,) representing the speed values for each joint of a 7-axis robotic arm in the format of a 4D array.

attributes.name: angles
attributes.description: Used to store the target angles for each joint of the robot, with values ranging from -0.5 to 0.5.

attributes.name: timer3
attributes.description: 4, indicating that the worker has been running for 4 seconds.

attributes.name: moveKinovaWithSpeeds
attributes.description: 3- indexed, representing the speed of movement for the Kinova robot's joints when the `move_mode` is set to 4. It takes into account the axis values from the `data` dictionary and moves the robot accordingly.

attributes.name: timer4
attributes.description: 4, indicating that the worker is a specific one with 4 possible states.

attributes.name: movePybulletWithToolbox
attributes.description: Used to move the end effector of a robot based on the data from the pybullet library, specifically the joint angles and button presses.

attributes.name: colorKinova
attributes.description: Used to represent the color of the robot's gripper or end effector.

attributes.name: depthKinova
attributes.description: 0 by default, representing the depth of the robot's end effector in Kinova units (where 1 unit = 0.05 meters).

attributes.name: calibrator
attributes.description: Used to store a reference to a calibration function that can be used to adjust the worker's joint angles based on sensor readings.

attributes.name: timer5
attributes.description: 5 by default, which represents the number of milliseconds to wait before checking if the worker should move to the next state based on the sensor data.

attributes.name: readKinovaCamera
attributes.description: Used to read camera data from a Kinova camera. It returns a list of dictionaries containing camera data, including position, orientation, and buttons.

items.name: SpecificWorker

items.description: Sets up the PyBullet and Kinova arm environments, initializes joint angles and velocities, and starts timers for computing and moving the robot.

params.name: proxy_map
params.description: Used to map the PyBullet joint angles to the corresponding Kinova arm joint angles. It allows for customization of the mapping between the two systems, which can be useful in certain scenarios where the exact mapping is not known or desired.

params.name: startup_check
params.description: Used to check if the kinova arm is properly initialized or not, if it's not properly initialized it will run the initialization code.

items.name: __init__

items.description: Sets parameters for an instance of the `SpecificWorker` class, which inherits from `GenericWorker`. The function returns `True` indicating successful parameter setting.

params.name: params
params.description: Passed to the method for modification or manipulation.

returns.description: True when successful and an exception otherwise.

items.name: setParams

items.description: Performs kinematic computations to move the robotic arm to observe specific angles, compute the toolbox, and calibrate the robot's end effector to a fixed cup position. It also reads camera images and displays them using OpenCV.
items.name: compute

items.description: Tests various aspects of the RoboCompKinovaArm, including its pose, gripper, joints, and joystick adapter. It also sets a timer to quit the application after 200 milliseconds.
items.name: startup_check

items.description: Computes the difference between the position and orientation of a cup estimated using the AAMED algorithm and the actual position and orientation of the cup, then adjusts the cup's position and orientation to minimize the difference.

returns.description: The absolute difference between the predicted position of the cup and the actual position obtained from the kinova API.

items.name: correctCupPosition

items.description: Initializes the toolbox of a SpecificWorker class by setting up the environment, kinematic chain, and end-effector pose, and defining the desired end-effector pose for manipulation tasks.
items.name: initialize_toolbox

items.description: Computes the joint velocities for a specific robot using a kinematic model and a servo controller. It updates the joint angles and the error between the desired and actual joint positions.
items.name: toolbox_compute

items.description: Defines camera intrinsic parameters and computes a view matrix using quaternion multiplication, then retrieves an image from the camera sensor.
items.name: read_camera_fixed

items.description: Retrieves camera data from Kinova and processes it to extract depth and color images, storing them in instance variables for later use.

returns.description: 1-dimensional numpy array containing a tuple of two elements: the first element is a 3D numpy array representing the depth image, and the second element is a float vector representing the alive time of the depth image.

items.name: readKinovaCamera

items.description: Computes and prints the angles of the kinova joints of an instance of `SpecificWorker`, which is a subclass of `GenericWorker`.
items.name: showKinovaAngles

items.description: Within the `SpecificWorker` class modifies the target velocities for joints specified by an external library, then applies motor control to move the robot at those velocities.
items.name: movePybulletWithExternalVel

items.description: Controls the velocity of a robot using PyBullet, setting joint motor control parameters to reach target velocities.
items.name: movePybulletWithToolbox

items.description: Continuously retrieves joint and gripper states from an external device, scales the gripper distance by 80%, and sleeps for 0.05 seconds before repeating the process.
items.name: readDataFromProxy

items.description: Moves the `kinovaarm_proxy` with angles calculated from angles inputted by user, and stores them into `self.angles.jointAngles`.

params.name: angles
params.description: 2D, representing the joint angles of the Kinova arm in degrees.

items.name: moveKinovaWithAngles

items.description: Calculates joint speeds for a Kinova arm based on input gains and applies them to the arm using the `moveJointsWithSpeed` method of the `kinovaarm_proxy` class.
items.name: moveKinovaWithSpeeds

items.description: Updates the gains for each joint in the robot based on the difference between the desired angle and the actual angle measured by the PyBullet simulator, and resets the `posesTimes` array to the current timestamp.
items.name: updateGains

items.description: Receives data from a joystick and updates the position and orientation of a robot based on the input values from the axes and buttons. It also updates the move mode and home positions of the robot.

params.name: data
params.description: Passed as an argument to the function, containing the data from the joystick inputs.

items.name: JoystickAdapter_sendData

structured.description: A custom QT class called RoboCompCameraRGBDSimple, which implements the CameraRGBDSimple interface from the ifaces module. The class provides three methods: getAll, getDepth, and getImage, which fetch and return depth, color, or both images and depth information from a camera stream. The code also defines several threading functions to handle the image and depth processing tasks.

items.description: Manages and processes video data from two sources, color and depth, using separate threads. It provides methods for getting images or depth information from the queues and returning them as iface objects.

attributes.name: Period
attributes.description: 100 by default, which represents the time interval between video frames captured by the camera.

attributes.name: hide
attributes.description: Used to hide the worker object from the Python interpreter, preventing it from being garbage collected.

attributes.name: depth_queue
attributes.description: Used to store depth images from the camera stream.

attributes.name: color_queue
attributes.description: Used to store the color frames captured by the camera. It is used to feed the color frames to the video display thread.

attributes.name: color_stream
attributes.description: A video capture object for capturing color images.

attributes.name: depth_stream
attributes.description: Used to capture depth images from a RTSP stream.

attributes.name: color_thread
attributes.description: Used to represent a thread that runs in parallel to the main thread, handling the color stream of the camera.

attributes.name: video_color_thread
attributes.description: Responsible for reading color frames from the video capture and adding them to a queue for processing.

attributes.name: depth_thread
attributes.description: Used to manage a separate thread for processing depth images.

attributes.name: video_depth_thread
attributes.description: Responsible for reading depth frames from a video capture device and storing them in a queue for processing.

attributes.name: startup_check
attributes.description: A method that checks if the RoboCompCameraRGBDSimple interface is available and functions correctly.

attributes.name: timer
attributes.description: Used to schedule a function call at a specific interval (in this case, every 100 milliseconds).

attributes.name: compute
attributes.description: Defined as a slot that will be called at regular intervals, as determined by the `Timer` object passed to its constructor. It has no effect on the code's behavior.

items.name: SpecificWorker

items.description: Initializes an instance of the SpecificWorker class by setting up video capture streams for color and depth, creating threads to process the streams, and starting a timer for computing depth values.

params.name: proxy_map
params.description: Used to store the proxy map for the SpecificWorker class.

params.name: startup_check
params.description: Used to check if the thread-based video reader has already been started.

items.name: __init__

items.description: Computes and returns a boolean value based on the visibility of an object.
items.name: compute

items.description: Reads frames from a video capture object `cap` and puts them into a queue `inqueue`. It continuously reads frames until the video is closed or an interrupt is encountered, at which point it prints "hilo finish" and releases the capture.

params.name: cap
params.description: Used to capture video frames from a video file or device.

params.name: inqueue
params.description: Used to store images read from the camera in a non-blocking manner.

items.name: video_color_thread

items.description: Reads frames from an opened Capture object `cap` and puts them into a queue `inqueue` until the queue is full, then it waits for the thread to retrieve the frame from the queue and repeats the process.

params.name: cap
params.description: Used to capture video frames from a video source.

params.name: inqueue
params.description: Used for putting frames from the camera into the queue for processing.

items.name: video_depth_thread

items.description: Performs startup checks for a RoboCompCameraRGBDSimple instance by printing the class name and creating instances of its child classes (TImage, TDepth, TRGBD). It also schedules the application to quit after 200 milliseconds.
items.name: startup_check

items.description: Retrieves and combines depth and color information from queues within the `SpecificWorker` class, returning a complete RGB-D image with alivetime metadata.

params.name: camera
params.description: Used to represent a RoboComp camera object.

returns.description: A RGBD image with depth and color information.

items.name: CameraRGBDSimple_getAll

items.description: Retrieves an RGB-D image from a depth queue and returns the corresponding depth value.

params.name: camera
params.description: A reference to a camera object.

returns.description: A struct containing height, width, and depth values of a image.

items.name: CameraRGBDSimple_getDepth

items.description: Retrieves an image from a color queue and returns a `TImage` object with height, width, and depth properties set to the image's dimensions, as well as the actual image data.

params.name: camera
params.description: Passed as an instance of RoboCompCameraRGBDSimple.

returns.description: A struct containing height, width and depth of an image along with the image itself.

items.name: CameraRGBDSimple_getImage
